title: senna pleurocarpa
intro: Senna pleurocarpa, also known as Sickle Bush, is a perennial legume plant native to Australia and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The plant's seeds and leaves contain anthraquinone glycosides, which are responsible for its laxative effects and have been used to treat various gastrointestinal issues. Senna pleurocarpa is often prepared as a tea or capsule and is also used in herbal remedies to promote digestion and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
